card_name: Top of His Game Son Gohan
ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly identifies Son Gohan. The art shows a Super Saiyan 2 adult male with lightning arcing around him in a tournament setting — exactly matching Gohan's canonical SS2 appearance at the 25th World Martial Arts Tournament where he was asked by Kibito to transform, only to be immediately drained by Spopovich and Yamu.

trivia_bullets.text: At the 25th World Martial Arts Tournament, Gohan agreed to demonstrate his Super Saiyan 2 transformation at Kibito's request, making himself an open target. Spopovich and Yamu immediately swarmed him — the drain scene is the narrative hinge connecting the tournament arc to the Babidi Saga. [CBR: World Tournament Saga; Dragon Ball Wiki]
sources: cbr.com: DBZ World Tournament Saga articles

trivia_bullets.text: The 'Top of His Game' title is ironic: at this moment in the story, Gohan WAS at his peak power but was caught completely off-guard. It marks his last true Super Saiyan 2 peak before the later series depowered him during his studies. Community discussions frequently note this tournament appearance as Gohan's narrative high-water mark in DBZ. [CBR: Dragon Ball Z Who Would Have Won the 25th Tournament]
sources: cbr.com: Dragon Ball Z Who Would Have Won the 25th World Martial Arts Tournament

trivia_bullets.text: In the TB02 set, Gohan cards typically carry the 'World Tournament' special trait — the set's thematic keyword linking all 25th Tenkaichi Budokai participants. Son Gohan is one of the higher-rarity pulls in the set, reflecting his narrative centrality during this arc. [dbs-cardgame.com TB02 product page]
